question_text: Mars takes approximately 687 Earth days to orbit the Sun. How many Mars years (Martian orbits) would pass in 2,058 Earth days?
explanation: To find how many Mars years pass, divide the total Earth days by the number of Earth days in one Mars year: 2,058 ÷ 687 = 3. This means 3 complete orbits of Mars around the Sun would occur in that time period.
